questions.question: What does SWR stand for?
questions.reason: SWR means Standing Wave Ratio and indicates how well the antenna system is matched.

questions.question: Why are feeder cables usually kept as short as practical?

options.text: To reduce signal loss in the cable

questions.reason: Longer feeders generally have greater loss, so keeping them reasonably short helps preserve signal power.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of mounting a VHF/UHF antenna higher?
questions.reason: Higher antennas usually improve line-of-sight paths and coverage at VHF/UHF.

questions.question: Which antenna polarization is commonly used for VHF/UHF FM mobile operation?
questions.reason: Vertical polarization is commonly used for FM mobile and many VHF/UHF local stations.

questions.question: What is one job of the outer braid or screen in coaxial cable?

options.text: It acts as a shield and return conductor

questions.reason: The outer conductor of coax acts as a screen and forms part of the transmission path.

questions.question: What is a likely result of a poorly matched antenna system?

options.text: Lower feeder loss in all cases

questions.reason: A mismatch between transmitter, feeder, and antenna commonly results in a higher SWR.

questions.question: Why is weatherproofing outdoor coax connectors important?

options.text: It helps prevent water ingress and increased losses
It removes the need for grounding

questions.reason: Moisture entering connectors can increase attenuation and degrade reliability.

questions.question: What is the usual purpose of a directional antenna?
questions.reason: Directional antennas provide gain by focusing radiation in specific directions.

questions.question: How does feeder loss generally change as frequency increases for similar coax length/type?
questions.reason: For most coax types, attenuation tends to be higher at higher frequencies.

questions.question: What is a practical reason to use a dummy load during transmitter testing?

options.text: To avoid radiating a signal over the air

questions.reason: A suitable dummy load allows testing while minimizing on-air transmissions.

questions.question: What is one common use of a balun in antenna systems?

options.text: To connect balanced and unbalanced systems

questions.reason: A balun is often used when interfacing balanced antennas with unbalanced feeder lines.

questions.question: Why can poor-quality or damaged coax connectors cause station problems?

options.text: They can introduce losses and intermittent faults
They reduce receiver sensitivity to zero

questions.reason: Bad connectors can add loss, mismatch, and intermittent connection issues.
